hypothesis: Incorporating temperature-dependent molecular conformational dynamics into foundation model pre-training—by augmenting SMILES corpora with molecular dynamics trajectory snapshots encoded as time-averaged structural fingerprints across a range of thermodynamic conditions—will enable SMILES-based foundation models to learn electrolyte solvation shell reorganization behavior, such that downstream predictors trained on these thermally-aware embeddings will achieve lower mean absolute error for ionic conductivity prediction across temperature-varied datasets compared to static SMILES embeddings, specifically because the current models' failure to capture concentration- and temperature-dependent ion-pairing transitions represents a quantifiable performance gap identified in this work.
